bids.reasoning: At the start of the negotiation, my goal is to maximize my own utility by making an offer with the highest possible value for each issue based on my preferences, as there is no time pressure or prior information about the opponent's preferences yet. Since this is the first offer, I will propose the highest utility values for all issues, which means offering valueD for all issues, because this combination will result in the maximum possible utility for me.

At this early stage of negotiation (t=0.02), I aim to maximize my utility by maintaining my initial offer with the highest utility values for each issue. The opponent's initial offer holds minimal utility for me as it involves values with the lowest utility across all issues. Their message emphasizes collaboration but does not change the strategic approach as their proposal heavily favors them. They have also yet to show any concessions, so I will hold firm on my high-utility offer for now to potentially improve future trade-offs.

At this early stage of the negotiation (t=0.04), the opponent is holding firm with an opening that offers minimal utility to me. Given the low time pressure, I will maintain my aspirational offer which maximizes my utility. This repeated bid indicates my firmness and signals my preference priorities strongly without any immediate need for concessive tradeoffs, as it's still very early in the negotiation process.

Although their messages convey a desire for collaboration, they have not altered their offering strategy. Therefore, I will continue to propose high-value combinations aligned with my preference while being mindful to consider alternate acceptable options if necessary as time progresses.
